page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the n4d server family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 CPU architecture and is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include any bundled local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses, which requires separate network configuration for external connectivity.

Operating within a balanced resource profile, the n4d-standard-8 offers a standard resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads. It is designed for applications requiring a steady allocation of dedicated compute resources without the need for specialized hardware acceleration. Typical workloads include web serving, application servers, microservices, and moderate database hosting.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-8 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the n4d server family, configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU hardware, necessitating external block storage solutions for data persistence. It also features zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed as a general-purpose resource, the n4d-standard-8 is engineered for workloads that require stable, dedicated compute performance without specialized hardware acceleration. It is well-suited for standard business applications, web serving, microservices, and mid-tier database deployments.
